name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash offers payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $4,000 with in-store and online applications at Memphis locations.
description_long:
Speedy Cash is a short-term lending storefront operating multiple locations in the Memphis, Tennessee area, including the Summer Avenue location profiled here. The company positions itself as a neighborhood lender offering fast access to emergency funds with transparent terms disclosed before loan signing. Their business model centers on same-day or next-day lending for consumers facing immediate cash needs, with both in-store and online application channels available. The Summer Avenue store operates extended hours (9am-7pm weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday, closed Sunday) to accommodate working customers.

The company offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (up to $425 with repayment due on or around the next paycheck), Lines of Credit (up to $4,000 with flexible draw and repayment over time), and Title Loans (up to $4,000 using vehicle collateral with clear title). All products require standard documentation including valid government ID, proof of employment/income, working phone number, email address, and proof of address. Title loans additionally require vehicle appraisal, proof of insurance, and clear title documentation. Beyond lending, Speedy Cash provides money services including money orders, wire transfers, check cashing, and Green Dot Visa debit card services.

What distinguishes Speedy Cash is their emphasis on in-store customer service and application transparency. The website explicitly states borrowers "will know exactly how much it costs to borrow the funds prior to signing" and encourages in-person visits where customers can ask questions throughout the process. The company operates nine locations across the Memphis area, providing neighborhood accessibility. Their line of credit product specifically addresses the limitation of fixed-amount payday loans by allowing customers to draw flexibly as needs fluctuate, with credit becoming available again as balances are paid down.

However, prospective borrowers should recognize that these are high-cost short-term lending products, particularly payday and title loans which typically carry APRs substantially exceeding 100% in most states. While the website emphasizes transparency and customer service, the underlying economics of payday and title lending mean costs accumulate rapidly. The company's maximum payday loan of $425 suggests this location operates under Tennessee lending regulations, which cap payday loan amounts. Customers should exhaust lower-cost alternatives (employer advances, credit union PALs, family loans) before pursuing these products.
address: Suites E&F, 375 Vann Dr Ste E, Jackson, TN 38305

company_info.headquarters: Suites E&F, 375 Vann Dr Ste E, Jackson, TN 38305

pros: Multiple loan options with varying limits ($425 payday, $4,000 line of credit, $4,000 title loan) to match different emergency needs
Nine Memphis-area locations plus online application option for accessibility and convenience
Extended business hours (9am-7pm most weekdays) accommodate working customers without requiring time off
Transparent pricing: company states borrowers know exact costs before signing loan agreement
Line of Credit product allows flexible draws up to approved limit as needs change, with credit replenishing as principal is paid
In-store application support with staff available to answer questions throughout process and discuss funding/repayment options
Additional money services (check cashing, wire transfers, money orders) available in-store beyond lending products
Same-day or next-day funding available through both in-store and online application channels
cons: Payday and title loans are high-cost products, typically carrying APRs exceeding 100% despite stated transparency—total cost of borrowing compounds quickly
Payday loans require repayment in full by next paycheck, creating risk of rollover debt for customers with tight cash flow
Title loans place vehicle at risk of repossession if payments are missed, requiring full vehicle insurance and clear title documentation
Website does not disclose specific APRs, fees, or finance charges—borrowers must visit store or call to learn actual costs before committing
Line of Credit up to $4,000 is still subject to approval based on employment and income verification; credit limits may be substantially lower than maximum advertised
best_for: Employed individuals with stable income facing genuine one-time emergencies (medical bills, car repairs, urgent expenses) who need funds within 24 hours
Vehicle owners with equity in their car who qualify for title loans and can afford repayment without risking vehicle loss
Customers needing flexible access to credit for extended or fluctuating expenses who qualify for the line of credit product
Customers already in the Memphis area with convenient store access who prefer in-person support and want to understand loan terms before signing
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for employed individuals facing genuine one-time emergencies who need same-day or next-day funding and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives (employer advances, credit union PALs, personal loans from banks or credit unions). The critical caveat is that payday and title loans are high-cost products—APRs typically exceed 100%—and borrowers should view them as emergency-only solutions, not regular financing tools. Customers considering title loans must fully understand the risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed.
services: Payday loans up to $425 with repayment on or around next paycheck
Line of credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw amounts and repayment over time
Title loans up to $4,000 using vehicle as collateral
In-store loan applications with staff support
Green Dot Visa debit card services
Vehicle appraisal for title loan qualification
Loan counseling and term explanation prior to application
